title: Inflammatory Skin Disorders: Monocyte-Derived Cells Take Center Stage
relevance: Directly discusses inflammatory monocyte-like cells in the Reynolds adult skin atlas context
findings: Inflammatory monocyte-like cells are CD14bright CD64bright CD163- and represent the major mononuclear phagocyte subset increased in lesional psoriasis and atopic dermatitis. They are likely not steady-state tissue-resident macrophages but derived from monocytes recruited during inflammation.
quotes: Monocytes recently recruited to inflamed skin have been referred to as monocyte-derived dendritic cells (Mo-DC), inflammatory DC, inflammatory monocyte-like (Infl Mo-like) cells, inflammatory Mf (Inf mac), or monocyte-derived Mf (Monomac) highlighting a need to better define their molecular identities and function
Monomacs displaying a monocyte-like molecular signature (CD14, S100A8, S100A9) comprise a mixture of Infl Mo-like MNP_1 and MNP_2 clusters identified by FCM
a cell state molecularly defined as Inf mac, but not Mac2 or Monomac, is a major subset that significantly increased in L psoriasis skin

dermal Inf mac which appear to express IL23A but not CD14 in adult healthy skin, and Monomacs displaying a monocyte-like molecular signature (CD14, S100A8, S100A9) comprise a mixture of Infl Mo-like MNP_1 and MNP_2 clusters identified by FCM

title: A rule-based data-informed cellular consensus map of the human mononuclear phagocyte cell space
relevance: Defines consensus marker genes for classical monocytes in single-cell data
findings: Classical CD14+ monocytes (the source population for inflammatory monocytes in tissues) are defined by expression of S100A8, S100A9, S100A12, VCAN, and FCN1 in consensus single-cell maps.
quotes: cluster nine expressed S100A8, S100A9 and S100A12 together with VCAN and FCN1, identifying them as classical CD14 + monocytes (Mono1/cMono)

title: Loss of T cell tolerance in the skin following immunopathology is linked to failed restoration of the dermal niche by recruited macrophages
relevance: Characterizes monocyte recruitment and differentiation in skin dermis
findings: Monocytes recruited to the dermis differentiate into immunomodulatory cells expressing inflammatory and regulatory cytokines. CD11c+CD14+ monocyte-derived macrophages expand in inflamed skin lesions.
quotes: dermal monocytes are specifically primed to become immunomodulatory cells that express classic inflammatory and regulatory cytokines
Focusing on cluster 1 demonstrated a sub-population of ITGAX + CD14 + S100A9 +
the dominant expansion of CD11c + CD14 + macrophages is a defining feature of the immune landscape within skin lesions

title: Immunology of Acute and Chronic Wound Healing
relevance: Reviews monocyte subsets and their roles in skin wound healing
findings: Classical CD14+CD16- pro-inflammatory monocytes are recruited to skin wounds where they differentiate into M1-type macrophages that produce inflammatory mediators. They play a critical role in the early inflammatory phase of wound healing.
quotes: Blood monocytes that are attracted to wounds are classified into classical or pro-inflammatory CD14 + CD16 - monocytes and anti-inflammatory CD14 low/- CD16 + monocytes
Pro-inflammatory macrophages produce inflammatory mediators, such as nitric oxide, ROS, IL-1, IL-6 and TNF-a, as well as MMP-2 and MMP-9

title: In Vivo Imaging Reveals a Pioneer Wave of Monocyte Recruitment into Mouse Skin Wounds
relevance: Demonstrates rapid inflammatory monocyte recruitment to skin wounds
findings: Classical CCR2+ inflammatory monocytes are rapidly recruited to skin wounds within hours of injury, as early as neutrophils, through active release from the bloodstream.
quotes: Within hours after the scalp injury, monocytes invaded the wound bed. The complete abrogation of this infiltration in monocyte-deficient CCR2-/- mice argues for the involvement of classical monocytes in this process
Monocyte infiltration unexpectedly occurred as early as neutrophil recruitment did

title: A prenatal skin atlas reveals immune regulation of human skin morphogenesis
relevance: The atlas paper that integrates adult skin data from Reynolds et al. with original annotations including Inf_mono
findings: The prenatal skin atlas integrated adult healthy skin data from Reynolds et al. 2021 with original annotations. Adult skin exhibits a more pro-inflammatory environment compared to prenatal skin.
quotes: All adult fibroblast subsets expressed high levels of inflammatory cytokines and receptors (for example, IL6 and IL1RA) and genes involved in antigen presentation (for example, HLA-A), innate immune and inflammatory responses (for example, CD55 and PTGES) and cellular senescence (CDKN1A)
